summary:
Excellent! The test passes now. The issue was that the `CsvReader` was using ISO-8859-1 encoding instead of UTF-8 to read the CSV file. When UTF-8 encoded text (which contains multi-byte characters for accented letters) is read as ISO-8859-1, each multi-byte UTF-8 character gets interpreted as multiple ISO-8859-1 characters, resulting in garbled output (mojibake).

The fix was simple: change the charset from `ISO-8859-1` to `UTF-8` in the `CsvReader` constructor. This ensures that the CSV file, which contains UTF-8 encoded international characters, is read correctly.

The needle was found in `app/CsvReader.java` line 17-20 where the wrong charset was being used.
